title: BCSI Destruction and Sanitization Evidence from ITAD Tickets

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-011-3 R4 Part 4.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-011-3 R4 Part 4.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.60: BCSI Destruction and Sanitization Evidence from ITAD Tickets.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.60' running on index=itsm sourcetype=itad:disposal, sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=itad:disposal status="completed" earliest=-365d
| rename asset_serial as serial
| join type=left serial [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(wipe_method) as method latest(cert_id) as cert by serial
  ]
| where bcsi_stored="true" AND isnull(cert)
| table disposal_id, serial, asset_tag, completed_at, cert
description: We correlate IT asset disposal tickets with wipe certificates indexed as structured events for sanitization evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes itad:disposal, itad:disposal`, itad:wipe_cert. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require vendors to upload certificate metadata to ITSM. (2) Alert when disposal closes without cert. (3) Store PDF hashes out-of-band if needed. (4) Annual sampling by internal audit. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=itad:disposal`, `sourcetype=itad:disposal``, `sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert`, `sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert``.
visualization: Table (missing certs), Single value (gap count), Timeline
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=itad:disposal`, `sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require vendors to upload certificate metadata to ITSM. (2) Alert when disposal closes without cert. (3) Store PDF hashes out-of-band if needed. (4) Annual sampling by internal audit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=itad:disposal status="completed" earliest=-365d
| rename asset_serial as serial
| join type=left serial [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(wipe_method) as method latest(cert_id) as cert by serial
  ]
| where bcsi_stored="true" AND isnull(cert)
| table disposal_id, serial, asset_tag, completed_at, cert
```

Understanding this SPL

**BCSI Destruction and Sanitization Evidence from ITAD Tickets (CIP-011-3 R4 Part 4.1)** — We correlate IT asset disposal tickets with wipe certificates indexed as structured events for sanitization evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=itad:disposal`, `sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: itad:disposal. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=itad:disposal, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where bcsi_stored="true" AND isnull(cert)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: asset_tag, cert, completed_at, disposal_id, method, serial.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing certs), Single value (gap count), Timeline
grandmaExplanation: We correlate IT asset disposal tickets with wipe certificates indexed as structured events for sanitization evidence. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
